title: 10 best herbal tinctures for tinnitus
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for tinnitus are liquid extracts made from plants, roots, and leaves that have been used for centuries to alleviate the symptoms of ringing in the ears. These natural remedies help by calming the auditory system, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation. For example, Ginkgo biloba, St. John's Wort, and Skullcap have been shown to improve tinnitus symptoms by reducing noise perception and anxiety levels. By incorporating these herbal tinctures into their daily routine, individuals with tinnitus may experience improved sleep quality, reduced stress, and increased overall well-being, allowing them to live a more fulfilling life free from the constant ringing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its unique composition, which includes flavonoids and terpenes that have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear, which can contribute to the development and exacerbation of tinnitus. The tincture's active ingredients also have been shown to improve blood flow and circulation to the ears, allowing for better communication between the auditory nerve and the brain and potentially reducing tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms.
Vasodilatory properties: The terpenes in Ginkgo biloba dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the ears and potentially reducing pressure and noise-induced damage that can cause tinnitus.

Neuroprotective effects: Ginkgo biloba's flavonoids have been shown to protect against neurotoxicity and promote neuronal health, which may help alleviate tinnitus symptoms by reducing noise sensitivity and improving auditory processing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote blood flow, making them effective in reducing tinnitus symptoms.

Barks: The bark of the maidenhair tree is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate tinnitus-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in large quantities ensuring organic and pesticide-free cultivation practices.
Wash the harvested ginkgo biloba leaves thoroughly with distilled water and let them air-dry for at least 24 hours.
Chop the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces using a food processor or sharp knife to facilitate extraction.
Combine the chopped ginkgo biloba leaves with 80% vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar at a ratio of 1:2.
Store the mixture in a dark glass container for four weeks, shaking the jar daily for optimal extraction and tincture potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort tinctures helps with tinnitus because it has been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, which is a common underlying cause of this condition. The herb also exhibits antioxidant properties, which can help protect the delicate hair cells in the inner ear from damage. Additionally, St John's wort has been found to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which may help alleviate the psychological distress associated with tinnitus. By addressing these underlying factors, herbal St John's wort tinctures may provide relief from tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: St John's Wort contains antioxidants that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, which may contribute to the development and progression of tinnitus.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The flavonoids present in St John's Wort have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the ear, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.

Neuroprotective compounds: St John's Wort contains neuroprotective compounds like hyperforin, which may help protect the inner ear nerves from damage and improve communication between them, potentially reducing tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, St John's Wort roots have been traditionally used to treat ear-related ailments, making them suitable for tinnitus relief.
Flowers: Containing a high concentration of hyperforin and hypericin, the flowers are often extracted to create tinctures that help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest hypericum perforatum flowers at peak bloom during sunny weather.
Cleanse the harvested flowers by gently removing excess dirt and debris.
Chop the cleaned hypericum perforatum flowers into small pieces using a sharp knife.
Steep the chopped hypericum perforatum in a base spirit such as vodka or everclear for 6 weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove solids and bottle for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its potent adaptogenic properties, which work to balance the nervous system and alleviate stress-related auditory disturbances. The herb's ability to enhance cognitive function, memory, and focus also plays a significant role in calming the mind and reducing mental chatter that can exacerbate tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, brahmi's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the ear, which can contribute to the development of tinnitus. By addressing these underlying factors, brahmi tinctures offer a natural and holistic approach to managing tinnitus.
remedy_properties: Cognitive Enhancing Properties: Brahmi's cognitive-enhancing properties can help to reduce stress, anxiety, and fatigue, which are common contributors to tinnitus, allowing the body to focus on other treatments.

Antioxidant Properties: Brahmi's antioxidant properties can help to protect the hair cells in the inner ear from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can exacerbate tinnitus.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Brahmi's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which is often a underlying cause of tinnitus, thereby alleviating symptoms such as ringing, buzzing, or roaring sounds.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain a high concentration of active compounds, including saponins and alkaloids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for treating tinnitus.

Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their rich content of bioactive molecules, such as saponins and glycosides, which have been reported to exhibit anticonvulsant and sedative effects that can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality bacopa monnieri leaves and flowers ensuring 20-30% bacosides content for optimal tinnitus relief.
Clean and dry the harvested herbs using air circulation or gentle heat to reduce moisture content below 10%.
Chop the dried herbs into small pieces then combine with a 40-50% ethanol solvent at a ratio of 1:4 for 12-hour maceration time.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a glass container after the maceration process to remove solids and retain tincture.
Filter the resulting tincture through an activated carbon filter or cotton gauze before bottling and storing in dark glass containers with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn tinctures helps with tinnitus because it has been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the auditory system. The bioactive compounds present in hawthorn, such as flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to alleviate the discomfort and distress associated with tinnitus. Additionally, hawthorn's vasodilatory effects may also help to improve oxygenation of the inner ear, reducing the perceived ringing or buzzing sounds that are characteristic of tinnitus.
remedy_properties: Cardiotonic effects: Hawthorn's cardiotonic properties help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which can alleviate tinnitus symptoms by reducing damage to hair cells.
Antioxidant activity: Hawthorn's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the inner ear, contributing to tinnitus development.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Hawthorn's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which can help to alleviate symptoms of tinnitus such as ringing, buzzing, or hissing sounds.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in flavonoids and anthocyanins, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve circulation, making them effective in alleviating tinnitus symptoms.

Barks: The barks contain a unique combination of bioactive compounds that help to strengthen blood vessels and improve cardiovascular health, both of which can contribute to the development and management of tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried c monogyna leaves and flowers in sufficient quantity for desired strength of tincture.
Grind 100g of dried material into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Combine 50% powder with 50% high-proof vodka in clean glass jar sealed tightly.
Allow mixture to macerate for 14 days shaking jar daily at same time each day.
Strain tincture through cheesecloth or coffee filter into dark glass bottle and store in cool place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its calming and balancing properties. The herbal extract has been shown to reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which can contribute to the development of tinnitus. Additionally, lavender's anxiolytic and sedative effects help to calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate tinnitus symptoms. By promoting relaxation and reducing auditory sensitivity, English lavender tinctures can provide relief from the constant ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: English lavender tincture's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to tinnitus by reducing damage to hair cells in the inner ear.

Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Lavender's anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and sedative effects can help alleviate symptoms of tinnitus such as anxiety, stress, and insomnia, which are often comorbid with tinnitus.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the inner ear, which can contribute to tinnitus by damaging or affecting hair cells responsible for sound processing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the English lavender plant contain a high concentration of essential oils and flavonoids, which have been shown to be effective in reducing tinnitus symptoms.
Seeds: Lavender seeds contain a unique combination of fatty acids and antioxidants that help to calm inflammation and reduce stress-related tinnitus.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in sufficient quantities for the desired yield.
Combine 100 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 500 milliliters of vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for eight to ten weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Transfer the final tincture to dark glass bottles and store at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its natural ability to calm and soothe the nervous system. The active compounds in valerian root, such as valerenic acid, work to reduce anxiety and stress levels, which are often linked to tinnitus symptoms. By promoting relaxation and reducing auditory hyperactivity, herbal valerian tinctures may help alleviate the ringing, buzzing, or hissing sounds associated with tinnitus, allowing individuals to experience a more peaceful and quiet environment.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) effects: Valerian's anxiolytic properties can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for tinnitus, allowing individuals to better cope with their symptoms.

Sedative properties: Valerian's sedative effects can help promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, both of which can be disrupted by the constant ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the inner ear, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in valerenic acid, a key compound responsible for the sedative and anxiolytic properties that help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.

Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes contain a high concentration of volatile oils, including isovaleric acid, which has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that can benefit tinnitus sufferers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality valeriana officinalis roots and dry them in a warm, dry place for at least 48 hours.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and pack them into clean glass jars leaving one inch of headspace.
Combine 40% vodka and 60% filtered water to create a solvent mixture and pour it over the packed root material.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its unique combination of natural compounds that target the underlying causes of this condition. The tincture's flavonoids and saponins work together to reduce inflammation in the auditory nerve, which can contribute to tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, maypop's antioxidants help to protect the hair cells in the ear from damage, allowing them to function properly and reducing the ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus. By addressing these underlying causes, maypop tinctures offer a natural solution for managing tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Maypop contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the ear, potentially alleviating Tinnitus symptoms.
Tannins: Maypop's high concentration of tannins can help constrict blood vessels and reduce swelling in the ears, which may relieve pressure and discomfort associated with Tinnitus.

Phenolic Acids: The phenolic acids present in Maypop have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help protect against ear damage and promote healing in individuals with Tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing a rich concentration of sesquiterpenes, maypop roots are valued for their ability to soothe and calm the auditory system, addressing tinnitus symptoms.

Barks: The inner bark of the maypop plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate tinnitus-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried passiflora incarnata flowers in the morning when they are fully open.
Combine one cup of dried flowers with 500 milliliters of 80% ethanol and allow it to steep for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 6 weeks before use allowing the flavors to mature.
Filter the tincture through a coffee filter or paper towel if sediment forms and transfer it to small dropper bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its ability to improve blood flow to the inner ear, which can help reduce ringing and buzzing sounds associated with the condition. The antioxidant properties in ginseng also help protect the delicate hair cells in the ear from damage, potentially reducing the intensity of tinnitus symptoms. Additionally, ginseng has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which may help alleviate stress and anxiety that can exacerbate tinnitus symptoms, providing relief for those affected by this condition.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic and Anti-Stress Properties: Ginseng tinctures have been shown to reduce stress and anxiety levels, which are often linked to tinnitus symptoms. By reducing stress, ginseng may help alleviate tinnitus-related discomfort.

Antioxidant Activity: Ginseng's antioxidant properties help protect the inner ear from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to tinnitus development and progression. By neutralizing these damaging compounds, ginseng may help reduce tinnitus symptoms.

Neuroprotective Properties: Ginseng has been found to possess neuroprotective compounds that may help prevent or slow down the progression of hearing loss and tinnitus-related auditory damage. This property may help preserve hearing function and alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary source of bioactive compounds responsible for the medicinal properties of ginseng, making them an ideal choice for tinnitus treatment.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, also known as underground stems, contain a concentrated amount of bioflavonoids and saponins that can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality panax ginseng roots and chop them into small pieces for optimal extraction.
Combine one part of chopped ginseng roots with two parts of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a dark place for 6-8 weeks to allow the tincture to steep.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding solids.
Store the final tincture in an amber glass bottle at room temperature away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with tinnitus because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce the inflammation and oxidative stress in the inner ear that may be contributing to the ringing or buzzing sounds associated with tinnitus. The bioactive compounds in licorice root, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, have been shown to possess potent protective effects on the auditory system, making them a potential natural remedy for managing this condition.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the ear and surrounding tissues, often associated with tinnitus.

Flavonoids: These bioactive compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the inner ear from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, potentially contributing to tinnitus symptoms.

Triterpenoid saponins: These molecules have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help reduce infections or inflammation in the middle ear that could be causing or exacerbating tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain glycyrrhizin, which helps reduce inflammation and promote healing in the inner ear.
Bark: The bark contains flavonoids and saponins, which have antioxidant properties and can help alleviate tinnitus symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean high-quality glycyrrhiza glabra roots, cutting them into small pieces for drying.
Dry the glycyrrhiza glabra root pieces in a warm, dry place or using a dehydrator for 24-48 hours.
Grind 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered glycyrrhiza glabra with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, stirring well to combine.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to extract the glycosides and flavonoids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with tinnitus because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development of tinnitus. By reducing inflammation in the auditory system, turmeric tinctures may help alleviate symptoms such as ringing, buzzing, or hissing sounds that are commonly associated with tinnitus. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of curcumin can protect against oxidative stress and damage to the auditory hair cells, which can further contribute to improved tinnitus management.
remedy_properties: Curcumin's Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Turmeric contains a powerful anti-inflammatory compound called curcumin, which can help reduce inflammation in the inner ear and auditory nerve, potentially alleviating tinnitus symptoms.
Turmeric's Antioxidant Activity: Turmeric's antioxidant properties can help protect the inner ear from damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to tinnitus development and progression.
Curcumin's Neuroprotective Effects: Curcumin has been shown to have neuroprotective effects, which may help prevent or slow down auditory nerve damage that can contribute to tinnitus.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, or underground stem, of turmeric contains a high concentration of curcuminoids, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in reducing tinnitus symptoms.
Roots: Turmeric roots have been used traditionally to treat various ear-related disorders, including tinnitus, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh curcuma longa turmeric roots at dawn or early morning for maximum potency.
Cut the turmeric roots into small pieces and dry them in a warm oven at 150°f for 3 hours.
Combine 50 grams of dried turmeric root powder with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining the liquid and discarding solids.
Transfer the filtered tincture to dark glass bottles, storing it in a cool place for future use as needed.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat tinnitus with herbal tinctures is to combine a few specific blends. Ginkgo biloba and St. John's Wort tinctures help alleviate ringing in the ears by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation. Valerian root and passionflower tinctures promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, which can exacerbate tinnitus symptoms. Use these tinctures in combination with regular dosing to experience optimal relief from tinnitus.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to tinnitus that are treated with herbal tinctures are conditions affecting the ear, nose, and throat, such as otitis media (middle ear inflammation), tonsillitis, and laryngitis. Herbal tinctures like Gelsemium sempervirens can help alleviate symptoms of hyperacusis, a condition characterized by an increased sensitivity to sound. Other herbs like Calendula officinalis and Echinacea angustifolia may aid in soothing ear infections and promoting overall auditory health.
